revision: EA-TCB-03 promoted to the unconditional assumption floor.

entries.statement: Legitimacy of the declared human-go approver root is assumed, not proven.
entries.forbidden_reading: a declared approver root is absolutely legitimate

entries.statement: The approver identity is assumed to be bound to the declared approver root and approval method.
entries.forbidden_reading: identity binding proves the approver was authorized for every action

entries.statement: Approval signature or approval-system verification depends on the correctness of the declared verification mechanism.
entries.forbidden_reading: a valid signature or approval-system check proves the action should run

entries.statement: Custody of approval credentials is assumed outside SPIRA.
entries.forbidden_reading: signature validity proves the credential was not compromised

entries.statement: Validity windows, freshness windows, nonce expiry, and revocation freshness depend on declared clock trust.
entries.forbidden_reading: SPIRA proves real-world time correctness

entries.statement: Clock-source identity and freshness are assumed according to the declared execution-authorization clock root.
entries.forbidden_reading: timestamp comparison proves the clock source is honest

entries.statement: Honesty and correctness of the human-go revocation source are assumed from the declared root.
entries.forbidden_reading: revocation check proves the source is honest

entries.statement: Unknown or stale revocation status is not evidence of non-revocation.
entries.forbidden_reading: unknown revocation status can support execution authorization

entries.statement: Correctness of the approver/operator role policy is assumed, not proven.
entries.forbidden_reading: role policy correctness is proven by SPIRA

entries.statement: Approver/operator coalescing is assumed valid only when explicitly declared by policy.
entries.forbidden_reading: silent role collapse is allowed

entries.statement: Correctness of the caller-supplied execution context is assumed outside the artifact under action.
entries.forbidden_reading: the target artifact may define its own execution context

entries.statement: Digest binding correctly represents the intended action, subject, environment, target scope, and side-effect summary.
entries.forbidden_reading: matching opaque digests alone prove the human understood the action

entries.statement: Rollback or abort channel availability is assumed from the declared rollback/abort reference.
entries.forbidden_reading: rollback reference proves rollback will succeed

entries.statement: Rollback or abort authority is distinct from execution authorization unless explicitly declared.
entries.forbidden_reading: permission to execute automatically grants permission to rollback or abort

entries.statement: A trusted verifier computes and compares execution-context digests independently of the runner or agent.
entries.forbidden_reading: the agent or runner may be trusted to validate its own authorization binding

entries.statement: Correctness and independence of the trusted verifier / TCB are assumed, not proven.
entries.forbidden_reading: SPIRA proves the verifier cannot be compromised

entries.statement: The trusted verifier observes the same action context that a future runner would receive.
entries.forbidden_reading: checking a prepared bundle proves the later runner input is identical

entries.statement: The human acknowledgement text is assumed to be readable and to describe the action, subject, target, side effects, and rollback/abort reference.
entries.forbidden_reading: a human approved a meaningful action by seeing only an opaque hash

entries.statement: The acknowledgement text digest is assumed to correspond to the exact text presented to the approver.
entries.forbidden_reading: digest binding proves the UI presented the text honestly

entries.statement: Human-go artifacts require one-time-use nonce or equivalent replay-prevention binding.
entries.forbidden_reading: a fresh approval may be reused for a different run

entries.statement: Nonce registry correctness and single-use enforcement are assumed from the declared nonce authority.
entries.forbidden_reading: SPIRA proves nonce storage cannot be bypassed

entries.statement: An authentic human go must not be applied to a different action, subject, target, environment, side-effect set, or evidence bundle.
entries.forbidden_reading: a real approval is valid for any similar-looking action

entries.statement: Confused-deputy prevention depends on the verifier comparing the human-approved context to the runner-intended context, not to a separately prepared bundle.
entries.forbidden_reading: approval authenticity alone prevents misapplication

entries.statement: Execution authorization is not execution.
entries.forbidden_reading: authorization evidence means the action was performed

entries.statement: Execution-authorization sufficiency is conditional on declared roots, the trusted verifier, and this ledger.
entries.forbidden_reading: sufficient means unconditional permission or safety

entries.statement: An execution-authorization result can only open a later runner gate; it does not authorize current runner implementation.
entries.forbidden_reading: a sufficient authorization model permits immediate runner code

forbidden_patterns: agent chooses both approved bundle and runner-intended bundle
verifier compares only prepared evidence bundle
verifier accepts copy-paste human text without digest binding

stability_rule: IDs are never deleted silently; removing or weakening an assumption requires a new ledger version and review.
